column3: We are well into TPC anniversary,
already in the fifth raid lockout of
phase 1, and likely we'll be getting a
tier 5 release date announcement within
the next four to 5 weeks. Being midway
through the first phase, I wanted to
pause and take a look at the current
state of Restoration Druid in TBC
Anniversary and see how the spec has
played out thus far. Has Resto Druid
performed up to expectations? Are people
playing Resto Druid? And more pointedly,
are too many people playing Resto Druid?
And what do resto druids have to look
forward to in phase 2? In a video I put
out in November last year, I shared how
resto druid is one of the top performers
in a raid setting, particularly in the
first two phases of TBC. Things do
change a bit in tier six when shamans
get their tier set bonus, which makes
chain heal even better though. But rest
performing towards the top of the
healing meters has definitely shown to
be the case in TBC anniversary as well
as the 2021 TBC Classic. on Warcraft
logs. Druid is only being beaten out by
Holy Priest, which I find to be pretty
unsurprising as Circle of Healing is
just so strong in TBC. I put out a raid
VOD of my second Resto Druid's first
Karazan raid where the tune had about
seven greens and was at,82 healing
power. In this level of gearing, I was
able to quote unquote beat out my shaman
healer counterpart who is actually
really strong when it comes to restoan
heal parses. And as I mentioned quote
unquote heal parsing, I do need to
include my disclaimer that heal parsing
is highly situational. It does not
provide a full scope of being a good
healer and that marks of being a good
healer really include making sure that
raiders don't die to normal mechanics,
that you make clutch decisions as a
healer to save players from their
mistakes and then being able to just
move efficiently through the raid, not
having a lot of downtime sitting and
drinking, but your raid is able to move
through uh the instance uh efficiently
and effectively. I really believe that
those are marks of good healing. much
more so than heal parses. But I bring up
this instance for Kerazan because it
also shows that when played correctly,
Restoration Druid is just a darn good
healer in TBC. So, has Resto Druid
performed up to my expectations thus
far? Definitely. TBC Resto Druid is
stronger and more fun than vanilla resto
druid in my opinion.

And if I, as someone who plays restorted
